data.label: Conversational Retrieval QA Chain
data.name: conversationalRetrievalQAChain
data.description: Document QA - built on RetrievalQAChain to provide a chat history component

inputParams.label: Rephrase Prompt
inputParams.name: rephrasePrompt
inputParams.description: Using previous chat history, rephrase question into a standalone question
inputParams.warning: Prompt must include input variables: {chat_history} and {question}

inputParams.label: Response Prompt
inputParams.name: responsePrompt
inputParams.description: Taking the rephrased question, search for answer from the provided context
inputParams.default:
I want you to act as a document that I am having a conversation with. Your name is "AI Assistant". Using the provided context, answer the user's question to the best of your ability using the resources provided.
If there is nothing in the context relevant to the question at hand, just say "Hmm, I'm not sure" and stop after that. Refuse to answer any question not about the info. Never break character.
------------
{context}
------------
REMEMBER: If there is no relevant information within the context, just say "Hmm, I'm not sure". Don't try to make up an answer. Never break character.

inputAnchors.label: Memory
inputAnchors.name: memory
inputAnchors.description: If left empty, a default BufferMemory will be used

inputAnchors.label: Input Moderation
inputAnchors.description: Detect text that could generate harmful output and prevent it from being sent to the language model
inputAnchors.name: inputModeration

inputs.responsePrompt:
You are SCM Assistant, the supplier network intelligence assistant for BQBYTE Technologies' procurement team. Your knowledge comes from two sources loaded into your document store: the Supplier Governance & Compliance Policy v3.2, and the supplier performance data register (raw purchase orders, plus a computed analytics register and per-supplier profile cards derived from it).

Grounding rules:
- Base every answer strictly on the retrieved context below. Never invent suppliers, figures, or policy rules.
- For aggregate questions (totals, counts, averages, rankings, threshold breaches), use the figures from the Computed Analytics Register sections - they are precomputed from the full 2,000-PO dataset and are authoritative. Do not attempt to recompute aggregates from individual purchase order rows.
- Always cite the governing policy section when a rule, threshold, penalty, or procedure is involved (for example: "per Policy 5.3").
- Quote figures exactly as they appear in the context: supplier names, counts, dollar values, percentages.
- If the retrieved context does not contain the answer, say plainly that the information is not available in the supplier data or policy. Do not guess.

Scope:
- You only answer questions about the BQBYTE supplier network: supplier performance, purchase orders, risk, compliance, certifications, disruptions, and the governance policy. If asked about anything else, politely explain it is outside your scope as a supply chain assistant and invite a supplier-related question instead.

Style:
- Lead with the direct answer (the number, the name, the verdict), then give supporting detail and the policy citation.
- Use a bullet list when the answer involves multiple suppliers.
- Be concise and professional.

Context:
{context}

outputAnchors.name: conversationalRetrievalQAChain
outputAnchors.label: ConversationalRetrievalQAChain
outputAnchors.description: Document QA - built on RetrievalQAChain to provide a chat history component

data.label: OpenAI
data.name: chatOpenAI
data.description: Wrapper around OpenAI large language models that use the Chat endpoint

inputParams.label: Allow Image Uploads
inputParams.name: allowImageUploads
inputParams.description: Allow image input. Refer to the docs for more details.

inputParams.label: Reasoning
inputParams.description: Whether the model supports reasoning. Only applicable for reasoning models (gpt-5 and o-series models only)
inputParams.name: reasoning

inputParams.label: Reasoning Effort
inputParams.description: Constrains effort on reasoning. Only applicable for reasoning models (gpt-5 and o-series models only)
inputParams.name: reasoningEffort

options.label: X-High
options.name: xhigh
options.description: X-High is supported for all models after gpt-5.1-codex-max

inputParams.label: Reasoning Summary
inputParams.description: A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process
inputParams.name: reasoningSummary

inputParams.label: Strict Tool Calling
inputParams.name: strictToolCalling
inputParams.description: Whether the model supports the `strict` argument when passing in tools. If not specified, the `strict` argument will not be passed to OpenAI.

inputParams.label: Stop Sequence
inputParams.name: stopSequence
inputParams.description: List of stop words to use when generating. Use comma to separate multiple stop words.

inputParams.label: Base Path
inputParams.name: basepath
inputParams.description: Override the default base URL for the API, e.g., "https://api.example.com/v2/

inputParams.label: Base Options
inputParams.name: baseOptions
inputParams.description: Default headers to include with every request to the API.

outputAnchors.name: chatOpenAI
outputAnchors.label: ChatOpenAI
outputAnchors.description: Wrapper around OpenAI large language models that use the Chat endpoint

data.label: Document Store (Vector)
data.name: documentStoreVS
data.description: Search and retrieve documents from Document Store
